source_book: NCERT Class 12 Ch.6 Electromagnetic Induction §6.9 Inductance (Self & Mutual, Exp 6.3) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. The atomic idea has two coupled halves. SELF-INDUCTANCE: a coil is electrical inertia — a changing current in it links a changing flux to itself (Λ = LI), so it induces a back-EMF ε_L = −L·dI/dt that opposes the CHANGE in its own current; the current therefore ramps and never jumps, L is pure geometry (turns N, area A, length l, core μ_r) and NEVER a function of the current, and the coil stores field energy U = ½LI² that escapes as the switch-off spark. MUTUAL INDUCTANCE: a changing current in one coil links a changing flux to a SECOND, disconnected coil (Λ = MI₁), inducing ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt across empty space with no wire between them; M = k√(L₁L₂) with k∈[0,1] falling with separation and rising with a shared core, and M is symmetric (M₁₂ = M₂₁). Both halves are the SAME Faraday flux-linkage physics — the transformer, the ignition coil, and the wireless charger all live here. Does NOT cover AC generators (next) or transformer voltage-ratio numerics. Built in the straightforward (non-Socratic) model: six motion-distinct teaching beats + one combined interactive state, each state exposing only its own contextual controls (Rule 31). Demo-compressed magnitudes: real RL time constants are milliseconds, stretched here to tau_demo = 0.6 s so the ramp and the spike are watchable; the RELATIONS are exact.

real_world_anchor.primary: Every motorcycle and scooter on an Indian road fires its spark plug using this concept. A coil carries a steady current from the battery; the moment the ignition circuit breaks that current, the coil's self-inductance fights the sudden drop with a huge back-EMF spike, and the mutually-coupled secondary winding steps that spike up to tens of thousands of volts — enough to jump the spark-plug gap and ignite the fuel. Break the current, get a spark: that is ½LI² of stored field energy escaping through self- and mutual inductance together.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Place a phone on a wireless charging pad and it charges with no metal contact. A changing current in the pad's coil induces an EMF in a coil inside the phone across the air gap — no wire between them. This is NCERT Experiment 6.3 sitting on a student's desk: two coils, magnetically coupled, energy crossing empty space.

I.name: Steady (final) current the coil ramps toward

N.name: Number of turns on the coil (sets self-inductance geometrically)

core.name: Core inside the single self-inductance coil (0=air, 1=iron)

L.name: Self-inductance of the coil
L.derived: L = L_ref*(N/N_ref)^2*(core==1?core_factor:1) [pure geometry, NEVER a function of I]

dIdt.name: Instantaneous rate of change of the coil current
dIdt.derived: switch-on:(I/tau_demo)*exp(-t/tau_demo); steady:0; switch-off:-(I/dt_arc); written back per frame by the renderer

U.name: Energy stored in the coil's magnetic field

d.name: Separation between the two coils (relative units)

core_shared.name: Shared iron core linking the two coils (0=none, 1=shared)

I1.name: Primary-coil current (oscillating drive for mutual induction)

dI1dt.name: Rate of change of the primary current

k.name: Coupling coefficient between the two coils (0=none, 1=perfect)

M.name: Mutual inductance between the two coils
M.derived: M = k*sqrt(L1*L2); L1=L2=L(N,core), identical demo coils, so M=k*L

emf2.name: EMF induced in the disconnected second coil

L_ref.name: reference self-inductance at N_ref, air core (demo scale)

N_ref.name: reference turn count for the demo L scale

core_factor.name: self-coil inductance boost with iron core (demo-compressed)

k_max.name: coupling coefficient at zero separation, no shared core

d_scale.name: separation decay length for the coupling coefficient

core_boost.name: coupling boost when a shared core is inserted

I1_amp.name: amplitude of the oscillating primary current

tau_demo.name: demo-compressed ramp time constant (real RL tau is ms; stretched to be watchable)

dt_arc.name: demo collapse time of the current at switch-off (sets the spark spike size)

formulas.self_emf: ε_L = −L·dI/dt — the coil opposes the CHANGE in its own current (electrical inertia)
formulas.self_inductance: L = μ₀·μ_r·N²·A/l — pure geometry: turns N, area A, length l, core μ_r; NEVER the current
formulas.energy_stored: U = ½·L·I² — energy held in the coil's magnetic field, released as the switch-off spark
formulas.mutual_emf: ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt — a changing primary current induces an EMF in a disconnected neighbour
formulas.mutual_inductance: M = k·√(L₁L₂), k∈[0,1]; k falls with separation d, rises with a shared core; M₁₂=M₂₁ (symmetric)

constraints: ε_L = −L·dI/dt: the back-EMF opposes the CHANGE in current, not the current itself
ε_L = 0 exactly when dI/dt = 0 (steady current), no matter how large the current I is
L depends only on geometry (N, area, length, core μ_r) — never on the current I or the EMF
The coil current is continuous — it ramps, it never jumps (a jump needs infinite dI/dt → infinite back-EMF)
U = ½LI² ≥ 0 always; the energy lives in the magnetic field and is released as the switch-off spark
ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt: a mutual EMF needs dI₁/dt ≠ 0, not merely a present current I₁ ≠ 0
M = k√(L₁L₂) with k∈[0,1]; k drops as d grows and rises with a shared core; M can never exceed √(L₁L₂)
M is symmetric: M₁₂ = M₂₁ — swapping primary and secondary gives the identical coupling
Self-induction and mutual induction are the SAME physics — Faraday's law on flux linkage (Λ=LI self, Λ=MI₁ mutual)

aha_moment.statement: A coil is electrical inertia: it fights any change in its own current.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: the ghost current jumps to full instantly while the real current beads refuse and ramp up smoothly, a red push-back arrow opposing the build-up.

STATE_1.title: A coil is electrical inertia: current ramps, it never jumps

scene_composition.text: ε_L = −L·dI/dt — the coil fights the change in its own current
ghost current jumps to full instantly (what a resistor would do — wrong)
real current ramps up smoothly — the coil refuses the jump

tts_sentences.text_en: Close the switch. A ghost current jumps straight to full — what a plain resistor would do; now watch the coil.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Switch बंद करिए। एक ghost current सीधे full पर jump कर जाती है — जैसा एक plain resistor करता; अब coil को देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Switch మూయండి. ఒక ghost current నేరుగా full కి jump అవుతుంది — ఒక plain resistor చేసేది అదే; ఇప్పుడు coil ని గమనించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The real current beads refuse to jump: they ramp up smoothly, and a red push-back arrow rises against the build-up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: असली current के beads jump करने से मना कर देते हैं: वे smoothly ramp up होते हैं, और एक red push-back arrow उस build-up के खिलाफ उठता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: నిజమైన current beads jump చేయడానికి నిరాకరిస్తాయి: అవి smoothly ramp up అవుతాయి, మరియు ఒక red push-back arrow ఆ build-up కి వ్యతిరేకంగా పైకి వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That push-back is the self back-EMF: ε_L equals minus self-inductance L times the rate of change of current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह push-back ही self back-EMF है: ε_L equals minus self-inductance L times the rate of change of current।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ push-backే self back-EMF: ε_L equals minus self-inductance L times the rate of change of current.

tts_sentences.text_en: A coil is electrical inertia: it fights any change in its own current. Steady current, and the push-back fades to zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक coil electrical inertia है: यह अपने खुद के current में किसी भी change से लड़ती है। Steady current आने पर push-back zero हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక coil అనేది electrical inertia: ఇది తన స్వంత current లో ఏ మార్పునైనా వ్యతిరేకిస్తుంది. Steady current వస్తే push-back zero కి తగ్గిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Current through an inductor jumps instantly to its full value, like current through a resistor.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: A ghost dashed current jumps to full at once (wrong), while the real current beads ramp up smoothly and a red push-back arrow opposes the build-up.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Current in a coil ramps continuously and never jumps — a jump would need infinite dI/dt, hence an infinite back-EMF.

STATE_2.title: Switch dynamics: back-EMF tracks dI/dt, not I — and the switch-off spark

scene_composition.text: steady current → dI/dt = 0 → ε_L = 0 (however large I is)
open the switch → dI/dt huge → ε_L spikes → spark
I-t and ε_L-t: back-EMF follows the RATE of change, not the current

tts_sentences.text_en: Put the current and back-EMF on live graphs. Close the switch: I ramps up, and ε_L bumps briefly then decays to zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current और back-EMF को live graphs पर रखिए। Switch बंद करिए: I ramp up होती है, और ε_L थोड़ी देर के लिए bump करके फिर zero पर decay हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current మరియు back-EMF ని live graphs మీద చూద్దాం. Switch మూయండి: I ramp up అవుతుంది, మరియు ε_L కొంచెం సేపు bump అయి తర్వాత zero కి decay అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Hold at steady current: I is large and flat, yet ε_L stays pinned at zero — it tracks the rate of change, not the current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Steady current पर रुकिए: I बड़ी और flat है, फिर भी ε_L zero पर ही pinned रहती है — यह current को नहीं, बल्कि rate of change को track करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Steady current దగ్గర ఆగండి: I పెద్దగా మరియు flat గా ఉంది, అయినా ε_L zero మీదే pinned గా ఉంటుంది — ఇది current ని కాదు, rate of change ని track చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now open the switch. The current collapses almost instantly, so the rate of change is enormous.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब switch खोलिए। Current लगभग instantly collapse हो जाती है, इसलिए rate of change बहुत enormous हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు switch తెరవండి. Current దాదాపు instantly collapse అవుతుంది, కాబట్టి rate of change చాలా enormous గా మారుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: ε_L spikes far above the battery, and a bright spark jumps the gap — the field energy escaping.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ε_L battery से बहुत ऊपर spike करती है, और एक bright spark gap को jump कर देती है — यह field energy का बाहर निकलना है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ε_L battery కంటే చాలా పైన spike అవుతుంది, మరియు ఒక bright spark gap ని jump చేస్తుంది — ఇది field energy బయటకు వెళ్ళడం.

misconception_watch.belief: The back-EMF opposes the current itself / a bigger steady current means a bigger back-EMF.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: At steady state the current trace is flat and LARGE while the ε_L trace sits pinned at ZERO; at switch-off the current collapse makes ε_L spike enormously.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The back-EMF tracks dI/dt, not I — at steady current it is exactly zero however large the current is, and it spikes when the current is cut.

STATE_3.title: What L depends on: pure geometry, never the current

scene_composition.text: L = μ₀ N² A / l — pure geometry, never the current
more turns N, or an iron core → bigger L (and a slower ramp)
drag the current I → L does not move at all

tts_sentences.text_en: A coil's self-inductance L is set by geometry alone. Add turns and L climbs with the square of N; slide an iron core in and L jumps many times over.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक coil की self-inductance L केवल geometry से तय होती है। Turns बढ़ाइए और L, N के square के साथ climb करती है; एक iron core अंदर slide करिए और L कई गुना jump कर जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక coil యొక్క self-inductance L కేవలం geometry వల్ల నిర్ణయించబడుతుంది. Turns పెంచండి మరియు L, N యొక్క square తో పాటు climb అవుతుంది; ఒక iron core లోపల slide చేయండి మరియు L చాలా రెట్లు jump అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A bigger L means more inertia — the same coil ramps up more slowly, now harder to change.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बड़ा L मतलब ज़्यादा inertia — वही coil अब और slowly ramp up होती है, अब change करना और कठिन हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: పెద్ద L అంటే ఎక్కువ inertia — అదే coil ఇప్పుడు మరింత slowly ramp up అవుతుంది, ఇప్పుడు మార్చడం మరింత కష్టంగా మారుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now drag the current itself: the beads speed up, but the L readout doesn't budge. Self-inductance L is pure geometry — never the current, never the back-EMF.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब current को खुद drag करिए: beads की speed बढ़ जाती है, लेकिन L का readout बिल्कुल नहीं हिलता। Self-inductance L पूरी तरह geometry पर निर्भर है — कभी current पर नहीं, कभी back-EMF पर नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు current నే drag చేయండి: beads వేగం పెరుగుతాయి, కానీ L యొక్క readout మాత్రం కదలదు. Self-inductance L అనేది pure geometry — ఎప్పుడూ current మీద కాదు, ఎప్పుడూ back-EMF మీద కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Inductance depends on the current flowing through the coil.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Dragging the current slider leaves the L readout fixed; adding turns or inserting the iron core is what makes L climb.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: L is pure geometry (turns, area, length, core) — it never depends on the current or the EMF.

STATE_4.title: Energy stored in the field: U = ½LI²

scene_composition.text: U = ½ L I² — energy stored in the coil's magnetic field
current rises → the reservoir fills (grows with I²)
cut the current → the field energy leaves as a spark

tts_sentences.text_en: Pushing current in works against the back-EMF, and that work is stored: energy U equals one-half L times current squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current को push करना back-EMF के विरुद्ध काम करता है, और वह काम store हो जाता है: energy U equals one-half L times current squared।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current ని push చేయడం back-EMF కి వ్యతిరేకంగా పని చేస్తుంది, మరియు ఆ పని store అవుతుంది: energy U equals one-half L times current squared.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the reservoir fill as the current rises — it grows with the square of the current, and it lives in the coil's magnetic field, not the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए जैसे-जैसे current बढ़ता है, reservoir भरता जाता है — यह current के square के साथ बढ़ता है, और यह wire में नहीं बल्कि coil के magnetic field में रहता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current పెరిగే కొద్దీ reservoir నిండటం గమనించండి — ఇది current యొక్క square తో పెరుగుతుంది, మరియు ఇది wire లో కాకుండా coil యొక్క magnetic field లో నివసిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Cut the current and the reservoir empties in a flash — that stored field energy is the switch-off spark, one-half L current squared escaping.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current को काटिए और reservoir एक पल में खाली हो जाता है — वह stored field energy ही switch-off की spark है, one-half L current squared बाहर निकल जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current ని తెగతెంపు చేయండి మరియు reservoir క్షణంలో ఖాళీ అవుతుంది — ఆ stored field energy యే switch-off spark, one-half L current squared బయటకు వెళ్ళిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: A coil at steady current stores no energy, or the energy is chemical energy inside the wire.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The reservoir fills as the current rises and empties into the spark when the current is cut — the energy lives in the field and grows as I².
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A current-carrying coil stores U = ½LI² in its magnetic field, released as the switch-off spark when the current is interrupted.

STATE_5.title: Mutual induction: an EMF across empty space, no wire between

scene_composition.text: A changing current in one coil induces an EMF in another — with no wire between.
air gap — no wire crosses it
ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt — the same flux-linkage physics as the self back-EMF

tts_sentences.text_en: Bring a second coil near the first — no wire, just an air gap. Drive an oscillating current I₁ through the first, so its flux keeps changing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक दूसरी coil को पहली के पास लाइए — कोई wire नहीं, बस एक air gap है। पहली coil में एक oscillating current I₁ drive करिए, ताकि उसका flux बदलता रहे।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండో coil ని మొదటి దాని దగ్గరకు తీసుకు రండి — wire ఏమీ లేదు, కేవలం ఒక air gap మాత్రమే. మొదటి coil లో ఒక oscillating current I₁ drive చేయండి, తద్వారా దాని flux మారుతూ ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That changing flux reaches across the gap into the second coil, and its galvanometer deflects — every cycle, no wire between them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह changing flux gap को पार करके दूसरी coil तक पहुँचता है, और उसका galvanometer deflect होता है — हर cycle में, बिना किसी wire के।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ changing flux gap ని దాటి రెండో coil లోకి చేరుతుంది, మరియు దాని galvanometer deflect అవుతుంది — ప్రతి cycle లో, వాటి మధ్య ఎటువంటి wire లేకుండా.

tts_sentences.text_en: The induced potential ε₂ equals minus mutual-inductance M times the rate of change of the primary current — the same flux-linkage physics as before.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Induced potential ε₂ equals minus mutual-inductance M times the rate of change of the primary current — यह वही flux-linkage physics है जो पहले भी थी।
tts_sentences.text_te: Induced potential ε₂ equals minus mutual-inductance M times the rate of change of the primary current — ఇది మనం ముందు చూసిన అదే flux-linkage physics.

tts_sentences.text_en: A changing current in one coil induces an EMF in another across empty space — the transformer and the wireless charger.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक coil में changing current, दूसरी coil में empty space के पार EMF induce करती है — यही transformer और wireless charger का आधार है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక coil లో changing current, మరొక coil లో empty space దాటి EMF induce చేస్తుంది — ఇదే transformer మరియు wireless charger యొక్క మూలం.

misconception_watch.belief: The two coils must be connected or touching for mutual inductance; self and mutual induction are unrelated phenomena.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: A highlighted air gap sits between the coils and the secondary galvanometer deflects with no wire crossing it; the induced-EMF arrow is drawn the same as the self back-EMF arrow.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Mutual induction needs only magnetic coupling across space, no wire — and it is the same Faraday flux-linkage physics as self-induction.

STATE_6.title: Coupling: M = k√(L₁L₂), distance, shared core, and reciprocity

scene_composition.text: M = k√(L₁L₂), k in [0,1] — falls with distance, rises with a shared core
move the coils apart → k and ε₂ shrink
swap primary and secondary → M is the same (M₁₂ = M₂₁)

tts_sentences.text_en: The coupling is set by M equals coupling-coefficient k times the square root of L-one L-two. Slide the coils apart and the linkage thins — k and ε₂ shrink.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह coupling M equals coupling-coefficient k times the square root of L-one L-two से तय होती है। Coils को अलग करिए और linkage कमज़ोर होती है — k और ε₂ दोनों घट जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ coupling M equals coupling-coefficient k times the square root of L-one L-two తో నిర్ణయించబడుతుంది. Coils ని దూరంగా జరపండి మరియు linkage తగ్గిపోతుంది — k మరియు ε₂ రెండూ తగ్గిపోతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Insert a shared iron core: the flux is funnelled across, k climbs, and ε₂ grows.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक shared iron core डालिए: flux उसके ज़रिए funnel हो जाता है, k बढ़ जाता है, और ε₂ भी बढ़ जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక shared iron core చొప్పించండి: flux అందులో నుండి funnel అవుతుంది, k పెరుగుతుంది, మరియు ε₂ కూడా పెరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Swap which coil is the primary — the deflection is identical. Mutual inductance is symmetric, M-one-two equals M-two-one.
tts_sentences.text_hi: कौन सी coil primary है इसे swap करने पर — deflection identical रहता है। Mutual inductance symmetric है, M-one-two equals M-two-one।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏ coil primary అని swap చేసినా — deflection identical గా ఉంటుంది. Mutual inductance symmetric, M-one-two equals M-two-one.

tts_sentences.text_en: So distance and a shared core set the coupling, and it never matters which coil you call the primary.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो distance और एक shared core coupling को set करते हैं, और इससे कोई फर्क नहीं पड़ता कि आप किस coil को primary कहते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి distance మరియు ఒక shared core coupling ని set చేస్తాయి, మరియు ఏ coil ని primary అని పిలుస్తారో దానితో ఎప్పటికీ సంబంధం ఉండదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Mutual inductance is different depending on which coil drives, and it does not depend on the gap or a shared core.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Sliding the coils apart shrinks k and ε₂; a shared core grows them; swapping primary and secondary gives the identical deflection.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: M = k√(L₁L₂) falls with distance and rises with a shared core, and it is symmetric: M₁₂ = M₂₁.

STATE_7.title: Explore: self and mutual inductance together

scene_composition.text: Set f, N, core, d, shared core — watch L, ε_L, U, M, ε₂
self: ε_L = −L·dI/dt, U = ½LI²   ·   mutual: ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt
L is geometry · M = k√(L₁L₂) · both are Faraday on flux linkage

tts_sentences.text_en: Now it's your turn — set the drive frequency, the turns, the core, the separation, and the shared core.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी है — drive frequency, turns, core, separation, और shared core को set करें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు — drive frequency, turns, core, separation, మరియు shared core ని set చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every quantity updates live: self-inductance L, back-EMF ε_L, stored energy U, mutual-inductance M, and the induced potential ε₂.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर quantity live update होती है: self-inductance L, back-EMF ε_L, stored energy U, mutual-inductance M, और induced potential ε₂।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి quantity live గా update అవుతుంది: self-inductance L, back-EMF ε_L, stored energy U, mutual-inductance M, మరియు induced potential ε₂.

tts_sentences.text_en: One idea ties it together: L is pure geometry, M is k times the root of L-one L-two, and both are Faraday's law on flux linkage.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक idea इसे एक साथ जोड़ता है: L pure geometry है, M है k times the root of L-one L-two, और दोनों flux linkage पर Faraday's law हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక idea దీన్ని కలిపి ఉంచుతుంది: L pure geometry, M k times the root of L-one L-two, మరియు రెండూ flux linkage పై Faraday's law.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered inductance when they can (1) describe a coil as electrical inertia whose current ramps continuously and never jumps; (2) state that the self back-EMF ε_L = −L·dI/dt tracks the RATE of change of current, so it is exactly zero at steady current however large the current is; (3) explain the switch-off voltage spike as the enormous dI/dt when the current is cut; (4) identify self-inductance L as a purely geometric property (turns, area, length, core) that never depends on the current; (5) state that a coil stores U = ½LI² of energy in its magnetic field, released as the switch-off spark; and (6) recognise mutual induction — a changing current in one coil inducing an EMF ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt in a disconnected neighbour across empty space, the transformer / wireless-charging principle, with M = k√(L₁L₂) symmetric between the coils.

questions.stem: A coil is connected to a battery through a switch. The instant you close the switch, what does the current in the coil do?

options.A: It ramps up smoothly toward its final value — it cannot jump, because the coil's self-inductance opposes any sudden change
options.B: It jumps instantly to its full final value, exactly like current through a plain resistor
options.C: It stays at zero forever, because a coil permanently blocks direct current
options.D: It overshoots well above the final value and then settles back down

distractor_misconceptions.B: treats the coil like a resistor, ignoring that a sudden jump would need infinite dI/dt and hence an infinite back-EMF
distractor_misconceptions.C: confuses an inductor's steady-state behaviour (it passes DC freely) with a permanent block
distractor_misconceptions.D: invents an overshoot with no basis; a simple current rise in a coil is monotonic, never above the final value

questions.tested_idea: A coil is electrical inertia: its self-inductance forces the current to ramp continuously and never jump.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain why the current through an inductor rises gradually after a switch is closed instead of appearing at its full value at once.

questions.stem: After the switch has been closed for a while, the coil carries a large, steady current. What is the back-EMF ε_L across the coil at that moment?

options.A: Exactly zero — the back-EMF depends on the rate of change of current, and a steady current has dI/dt = 0
options.B: Large, because the current is large — a bigger current means a bigger back-EMF
options.C: Equal to the battery voltage, which is what holds the current steady

distractor_misconceptions.B: believes the back-EMF tracks the current I itself rather than its rate of change dI/dt
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines the back-EMF must balance the battery even at steady state, confusing a resistor's IR drop with an inductor's back-EMF
distractor_misconceptions.D: guesses an arbitrary fraction of the source voltage with no physical basis

questions.tested_idea: ε_L = −L·dI/dt, so the back-EMF is exactly zero at steady current no matter how large the current is.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A coil carries a constant 5 A. State its back-EMF and justify your answer in terms of dI/dt.

questions.stem: You open the switch on a coil that was carrying a steady current, and a bright spark jumps the opening contacts — a spark far bigger than the battery's own voltage. Why?

options.A: Opening the switch forces the current to collapse almost instantly, so dI/dt is enormous and the coil's back-EMF (−L·dI/dt) spikes to a very high voltage
options.B: The battery voltage suddenly increases the moment the switch opens
options.C: The spark is just leftover current leaking through the air; it has nothing to do with the coil
options.D: Opening the switch has no real electrical effect; the spark is static electricity from your hand

distractor_misconceptions.B: blames the battery, missing that the coil itself generates the high-voltage spike
distractor_misconceptions.C: ignores the coil's role and the ½LI² field energy that must go somewhere when the current is cut
distractor_misconceptions.D: dismisses the effect entirely, not seeing the self-induced high-voltage transient

questions.tested_idea: Cutting the current fast makes dI/dt huge, so the self-induced back-EMF spikes far above the source voltage — the switch-off spark.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain why breaking the current in an inductive circuit can produce a voltage spike many times larger than the supply voltage.

questions.stem: Which change increases a coil's self-inductance L?

options.A: Adding turns or inserting an iron core — L is set purely by geometry (turns, area, length, core), never by the current
options.B: Increasing the current flowing through the coil
options.C: Increasing the back-EMF across the coil
options.D: Nothing changes L; it is a fixed universal constant like the speed of light

distractor_misconceptions.B: believes L depends on the current, the classic self-inductance misconception
distractor_misconceptions.C: reverses cause and effect — the back-EMF is a result of L and dI/dt, not a cause of L
distractor_misconceptions.D: treats L as a universal constant rather than a property of a particular coil's geometry

questions.tested_idea: Self-inductance L depends only on the coil's geometry (turns, area, length, core), never on the current or the EMF.
questions.parallel_form_stem: List the physical changes to a coil that raise its self-inductance, and explain why changing the current is not one of them.

questions.stem: A coil of inductance L carries a steady current I. How much energy is stored, and where does it reside?

options.A: U = ½LI², stored in the coil's magnetic field — and it is released as the switch-off spark
options.B: U = LI², stored as chemical energy inside the coil's wire
options.C: Zero — a coil carrying a steady current stores no energy
options.D: U = ½CV², the same formula as a capacitor

distractor_misconceptions.B: drops the factor of ½ and misplaces the energy as chemical energy rather than magnetic-field energy
distractor_misconceptions.C: confuses 'no back-EMF at steady current' with 'no stored energy', missing the field energy
distractor_misconceptions.D: applies the capacitor energy formula to an inductor

questions.tested_idea: A current-carrying coil stores U = ½LI² in its magnetic field, released when the current is interrupted.
questions.parallel_form_stem: State the energy stored in an inductor carrying current I, and explain where that energy physically resides.

questions.stem: Two coils sit near each other with no wire connecting them. You change the current in the first coil. What happens in the second, disconnected coil?

options.A: An EMF is induced in it — a changing current in one coil induces an EMF in the other across empty space, with no wire between them
options.B: Nothing, because the two coils are not electrically connected
options.C: An EMF appears only if the first coil's current is large, not merely if it is changing
options.D: The second coil heats up but develops no EMF

distractor_misconceptions.B: insists a wire connection is required, missing that magnetic coupling crosses empty space
distractor_misconceptions.C: believes a present current I₁ rather than its change dI₁/dt drives the mutual EMF
distractor_misconceptions.D: invents a heating-only effect, ignoring the induced EMF from the changing flux linkage

questions.tested_idea: A changing current in one coil induces an EMF in a magnetically coupled neighbour with no wire between them (mutual induction, ε₂ = −M·dI₁/dt).
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two nearby coils share no wire. Explain how changing the current in one produces a voltage in the other.

STATE_1.label: Electrical inertia: ghost jump vs real ramp
STATE_1.caption: Ghost current jumps instantly (wrong); the real current ramps smoothly and a red push-back arrow opposes the build-up.

STATE_2.label: Switch dynamics: ε_L tracks dI/dt, not I — and the switch-off spark
STATE_2.caption: Steady current → ε_L = 0 (however large I). Open the switch → dI/dt huge → ε_L spikes → spark.

STATE_3.label: What L depends on: pure geometry, never the current
STATE_3.caption: More turns N or an iron core → bigger L. Drag the current I → the L readout does not move.

STATE_4.label: Energy stored in the field: U = ½LI²
STATE_4.caption: The reservoir fills as the current rises (∝ I²) and empties into a spark when the current is cut.

STATE_5.label: Mutual induction: an EMF across the air gap, no wire
STATE_5.caption: A changing primary current I₁ sends flux across the air gap; the secondary needle deflects with no wire between them.

STATE_6.label: Coupling: distance, shared core, and reciprocity
STATE_6.caption: Move the coils apart → k and ε₂ shrink. A shared core grows them. Swap primary/secondary → M is the same.

STATE_7.label: Explore: self and mutual inductance together
STATE_7.caption: Set I, f, N, core, d, shared core — the self coil (top) and the mutual pair (below) update L, ε_L, U, M, ε₂ live.
